posts.title: Extract PDF text in your browser with LiteParse for the web
posts.author: Simon Willison
posts.summary: LlamaIndex have a most excellent open source project called LiteParse, which provides a Node.js CLI tool for extracting text from PDFs. I got a version of LiteParse working entirely in the browser, using most of the same libraries that...
posts.highlights: LiteParse is a Node.js CLI tool for extracting text from PDFs, and this post shows how to run it entirely in the browser using the same libraries. The key insight is that many server-side tools can be adapted for client-side execution, enabling new interactive applications without backend dependencies.
posts.worthReading: It demonstrates a practical approach to porting a server-side tool to the browser, which is valuable for developers looking to build offline-capable or low-latency PDF processing features.

posts.title: A pelican for GPT-5.5 via the semi-official Codex backdoor API
posts.author: Simon Willison
posts.summary: GPT-5.5 is out. It's available in OpenAI Codex and is rolling out to paid ChatGPT subscribers. I've had some preview access and found it to be a fast, effective and highly capable model. As is usually the case these days, it's hard to...
posts.highlights: GPT-5.5 is now available via OpenAI Codex and rolling out to paid ChatGPT users. The author finds it fast, effective, and highly capable, with improvements in coding and reasoning tasks.
posts.worthReading: Simon Willison provides early hands-on impressions of GPT-5.5, highlighting its performance and the novel 'Codex backdoor' access method, which is valuable for developers tracking OpenAI's latest model capabilities.
